'''Sodarra''' was a loyalist [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of an elite [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] unit. Sodarra's loyalty to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] put him in conflict with the renegade Imperial [[Governor-General/Legends|Governor-General]] [[Nox Vellam]] in the aftermath of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].

==Biography==
As a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the loyalist Sodarra commanded a unit of elite [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], including [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Birdloe]] and trooper [[CV-481]]. Although Sodarra trusted Birdloe, his second-in-command, for his impeccable record, the lieutenant was in reality a spy for the treacherous [[Governor-General/Legends|Governor-General]] [[Nox Vellam]].<ref name="Scoundrel" />

After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Sodarra accompanied [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to [[Vaal/Legends|Vaal]] to recover the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith's]] damaged [[Darth Vader's TIE Advanced/Legends|TIE fighter]], left there in the battle's aftermath. They soon retrieved the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]],<ref name="Vader">''[[Vader: The Ultimate Guide]]''</ref> but through the machinations of Vellam, a spiteful renegade Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]] envious of Vader's influence with the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]], the Dark Lord was rendered unconscious within its [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]. With its wings and support pylons severed, the cockpit was loaded into a [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Plasteel/Legends|plasteel]] crate. With Vader's body in his possession, Sodarra and his men fled from Vellam's forces in an Imperial-marked [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]]. With Vellam's [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Eradicator (Imperial-class)|''Eradicator'']] upon them in the [[Aldo Spachian system]], Sodarra's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] crash-landed on the nucleus of the [[Aldo Spachian Comet]], broadcasting a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress signal]].<ref name="Scoundrel" />

The signal reached [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], captain of the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], who was embroiled in a race to rescue [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leader [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] from the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Alfreda Goot]]. Pursued by ''Eradicator''{{'s}} [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] complement, Solo landed on the [[Comet/Legends|comet's]] surface, believing Organa's ship to be source of the distress call. Donning a [[Environment suit/Legends|vac-suit]], Sodarra and three of his men made contact with the surprised Solo, and, in a ploy to secure Solo's aid, Sodarra passed himself and his men off as Imperial deserters, hunted by their former compatriots for attempting to sell a prototype [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] to a double-crossing [[Ploovo Two-For-One/Legends|Ploovo Two-For-One]] rather than transport it to secret [[Imperial Production Laboratories]] on [[Rigoron]]. Solo agreed to help in exchange for four shares in the sale of the supposed device, and Sodarra, his six troopers, and Vader's crate—ostensibly containing the cloaking device—were brought aboard the ''Falcon'' as Vellam's TIEs arrived. With Sodarra's stormtroopers manning the ship's guns, Solo took the ''Falcon'' into the shelter of the comet's gaseous tail, escaping the Star Destroyer and its TIEs only to encounter Alfreda Goot, who reiterated the terms of the race, and made a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump towards its destination of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="Scoundrel" />

While Sodarra was familiar with Alfreda Goot from her work for Vader, he did not dissuade Solo of his illusions that Goot was in fact Organa in disguise, instead trivializing the race and encouraging Solo to bring him, his men, and their cargo to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Shador]], where he hoped to make contact with Darth Vader's [[Togorian/Legends|Togorian]] agent [[Taslo Deville]]. As Vellam's forces had been discovered to be following the ''Falcon'' via its faulty transmission unit, Sodarra suggested that diverting from the race to deliver his group would free Solo from the governor's pursuit. However, Han was unwilling to risk Leia further, and followed Goot into hyperspace. Sodarra resisted his suggestion that the cloaking device be employed to hide them from Vellam, claiming that the ''Falcon''{{'s}} reactor lacked the necessary strength, only for Solo to discover the crate's true contents, and Vader's presence. His ruse revealed, Sodarra forced Solo to bring the party to Shador, though Solo and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] copilot [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] were able to plant a [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]] on Vader's cockpit.<ref name="Scoundrel" />

However, the ''Falcon''{{'s}} movements had been reported to Vellam by the traitorous Lieutenant Birdloe, and the governor's forces assassinated Taslo Deville in his [[Fink's Hole]] residence prior to the ship's arrival. Berthing at Shador's [[Mud Station]], Sodarra received to no communications from the agent, and set out with Solo to Fink's Hole, ordering his men to kill Solo if he returned alone. In [[Zeboron Gamma]], Deville's residence, Solo and Sodarra engaged and killed his assassins, a group of human and [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. [[Standard hour/Legends|Hours]] later, after a thorough and fruitless search for answers, Sodarra was lead by Solo back to the ''Falcon'', stealing a [[Boat/Legends|boat]] to evade a group of Vellam's stormtroopers. Leaving the planet just as ''Eradicator'' arrived, Solo revealed the bomb on Vader's cockpit, threatening to evacuate the ''Falcon''{{'s}} cargo hold and activate it, killing the Dark Lord. Sodarra countered with a revelation of his own—that Alfreda Goot was in fact a bounty hunter and ruthless killer, and not Princess Leia in disguise. Solo agreed to spare Vader's life in exchange for Sodarra's support in the princess' rescue, before tricking Birdloe into revealing himself as a traitor, launching an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] which Solo then destroyed, having already deduced that one of Sodarra's men had arranged for the events on Shador.<ref name="Scoundrel" />

Travelling through hyperspace, the ''Falcon'' arrived at Tatooine, while Sodarra expressed his surprise that Birdloe had been an agent of Vellam. Landing at [[Bay 32]] in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], Sodarra volunteered to investigate the [[Dockside Cafe]], the destination provided by Alfreda Goot. As Solo and Chewbacca entered the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], Sodarra and his stormtroopers waited outside, hoping to take Goot by surprise. After spotting a group of [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] recruited by the bounty hunter as backup, Solo gave Sodarra the signal to enter, triggering a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fight with Goot's men and other aggressive patrons. Sodarra and his men cornered the aliens as Solo pursued Alfreda Goot—with a captive Princess Leia in tow—into a stockroom. With Goot dead and Leia free, the outraged princess questioned Solo's alliance with Imperial forces, prompting Sodarra to claim that the conditions of their agreement prohibited its discussion and to remark privately to Solo that the pair would suffer equally from the affair's discovery. Despite Sodarra's developed respect for Solo, he, Organa, and Chewbacca were forced to flee in the ''Millennium Falcon'' as the captain betrayed him and summoned Imperial reinforcements.<ref name="Scoundrel" />

==Personality and traits==
A short and stocky [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Sodarra was a loyal Imperial, unflinching towards the deaths of traitors and reluctant to forego his [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|officer's uniform]], even on such Empire-unfriendly worlds as Shador. Sodarra was out of place on Shador, where he was susceptible to losing his sense of direction in its labyrinthine canals, and was perturbed by the planet's unusual odors and creatures. While he regarded Han Solo as a worthy ally, offering to petition his pardon from Darth Vader, he was well-versed in deception and willing to lie to and betray Solo to secure his cooperation.<ref name="Scoundrel" />

==Behind the scenes==
Sodarra first appeared in the choose-your-own adventure book ''[[Scoundrel's Luck]]'', written by [[Troy Denning]] and published by [[West End Games]].<ref name="Scoundrel" />

''Scoundrel's Luck'' presents many branching paths with a variety of outcomes, some of which result in the death of Sodarra.<ref name="Scoundrel" /> However, the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']] establishes that Goot was able to lure Solo to the Dockside Cafe, but was ultimately unsuccessful in her hunt,<ref name="HM">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> an outcome contingent on Sodarra's survival.

Other potential paths involve Sodarra's corvette being boarded by ''Eradicator'' or crashing on the planet [[Mon Torri]], then working with Solo to escape [[Cold assault stormtrooper|snowtroopers]] and [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT walkers]]. These events occur only if Solo takes a shortcut through the [[Mon Torri system/Legends|Mon Torri system]],<ref name="Scoundrel" /> while [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Kendal Ozzel's]] entry in the ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' establishes that the canonical path is that in which he takes a longer route through the [[Black Widow Nebula/Legends|Black Widow Nebula]].<ref name="Ozzel">{{CSWECite|II|433|Ozzel, Admiral Kendal}}</ref>
